Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Complex The Leviathan Australian Intrusions group has been identified as a sophisticated threat actor targeting the communications industry. Their attack chain involves multiple complex steps, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Active Directory User Account Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Scheduled Task Target System Artifact Process File Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method Custom Command and Control Protocol PowerShell Script Scheduled Task/Job Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility File Access (Mechanism) Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about the targeted organization's network architecture using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker creates a compromised infrastructure for future use by compromising internet scans.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker targets vulnerabilities in publicly exposed applications to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker uses malicious websites to compromise users' devices.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attacker establishes communication channels between compromised systems using custom command and control protocols.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker utilizes PowerShell for command execution and persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistent access.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker elevates privileges to gain control over the system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.technique_name: T1198: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking
stage.description: The attacker disables antivirus software to evade detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker gains unauthorized access using brute force attacks.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker identifies potential targets within the network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses legitimate accounts to move laterally through the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker transfers stolen data out of the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical services to cause maximum damage.

Scenario.summary: The Leviathan Australian Intrusions group employs a complex attack chain involving multiple techniques to compromise the communications industry. They gather information, develop resources, establish initial access, execute commands, persist, escalate privileges, evade defenses, access credentials, discover targets, move laterally, collect data, exfiltrate data, and finally cause impact.
